patch.value: ADR: profile lint severity model — errors break apply, warnings flag suspicious-but-valid cross-references

Design decision for lintProjectProfile (pm-j1fj). ERRORS are conditions that make 'pm profile apply' throw or silently lose data: invalid/empty type, status, or field (normalizer throws — empty id, builtin-status override, builtin-field shadow), duplicate normalized keys within a dimension (silent last-wins upsert), unknown/invalid config knob (planConfig throws at apply), empty profile name (resolution requires it), and malformed workflow transition pairs (silently dropped by resolveTypeWorkflows). WARNINGS are authored content that is structurally valid but likely a bug: workflow.type referencing a type neither builtin nor profile-declared, workflow transitions referencing a status token (id/alias) neither builtin nor profile-declared (a dead/unreachable rule), template options.type referencing an undeclared type, non-normalized profile name (resolution folds case/hyphen but stored/display name differs), empty title/summary, and empty package spec. Warnings (not errors) for cross-references because the target type/status MAY pre-exist in the tracker independent of the profile, so erroring would create false positives against valid layered setups. assertProjectProfile throws on errorCount>0 by default; strict:true also throws on warnings for CI gates.

## ADR: Profile lint severity model

### Status
Accepted — implemented in PR #404 (`src/core/profile/profile-lint.ts`), exercised by `assertProjectProfile` and `pm profile lint`.

### Context
`lintProjectProfile` grades findings so that `pm profile apply` never silently loses data and authors get early signal on suspicious-but-valid content. A single severity would either block valid layered setups (false positives) or let data-loss conditions through.

### Decision
- **Errors** are conditions that make `pm profile apply` throw or silently drop data: invalid/empty type, status, or field (normalizer throws on empty id, builtin-status override, builtin-field shadow); duplicate normalized keys within a dimension (silent last-wins upsert); unknown/invalid config knob (planConfig throws at apply); empty profile name (resolution requires it); malformed workflow transition pairs (silently dropped by resolveTypeWorkflows).
- **Warnings** are structurally valid but likely-bug content: a workflow `type` neither builtin nor profile-declared; workflow transitions referencing a status token neither builtin nor profile-declared (dead/unreachable rule); a template `options.type` referencing an undeclared type; a non-normalized profile name; empty title/summary; empty package spec. Cross-references are warnings (not errors) because the target type/status may pre-exist in the tracker independent of the profile, so erroring would create false positives against valid layered setups.
- `assertProjectProfile` throws on `errorCount > 0` by default; `strict: true` also throws on warnings for CI gates.

### Consequences
Apply-breaking and data-loss conditions are caught at author time with stable codes, while layered/extension setups are not falsely rejected; `strict` mode gives CI a zero-warning bar. Implemented and covered at 100% in #404.

value.note: implements the graded error/warning severity model this ADR records

patch.value: ADR formalized and accepted: the profile lint severity model (errors break apply / silently lose data; warnings flag structurally-valid suspicious cross-references; assertProjectProfile throws on errors, strict also on warnings) is recorded in the item body and implemented in src/core/profile/profile-lint.ts as shipped in PR #404.
Recorded the accepted ADR for the profile lint severity model in the item body (Context/Decision/Consequences) reflecting the implementation shipped in PR #404.

A durable decision record explaining why lintProjectProfile grades findings as errors (apply-breaking / data-loss) versus warnings (structurally-valid suspicious cross-references), and how assertProjectProfile gates them.
ADR body added and accepted; the model is implemented in src/core/profile/profile-lint.ts and enforced by assertProjectProfile (throws on errors; strict also on warnings), covered at 100% in #404.
